cards.front: What is the Pumping Lemma for?
cards.back: A proof that a language is not context-free

cards.front: What is the Pumping Lemma for Context-Free Languages?
cards.back: If a language is context-free, then there exists a constant p such that any string w of length at least p can be divided into five substrings (u, v, w, v', w') such that |v| > 0, |v'| > 0, and uv^iw' is a valid string of the language

cards.front: What is the purpose of the Pumping Lemma?
cards.back: To prove that a language cannot be context-free by showing that it violates the pumping property
A string w of the language can be divided into five substrings (u, v, w, v', w') such that |v| > 0, |v'| > 0, and uv^iw' is a valid string of the language

cards.front: Is the Pumping Lemma a sufficient test for a language to be context-free?
cards.back: No, it is a necessary but not sufficient test. A language can pass the Pumping Lemma but still not be context-free
